hero.sub: Virgin wool · Natural dyes · Hand embroidered

brand.line1: From Quellón, Chiloé, every garment is born from the traditional Chilote loom.
brand.line2: Virgin sheep wool, dyed with southern herbs, hand-embroidered by María Ruth.
brand.line3: Each piece is one of a kind — you carry a piece of Isla Grande with you.

craft.title: The Craft
craft.p1: The Chilote loom is an ancestral technique brought by Spanish settlers to the Chiloé archipelago. At Ayenantú, we keep that tradition alive using wooden looms to weave every garment from scratch.
craft.p2: The wool comes directly from sheep raised on Isla Grande. We dye it with native plants — maqui, tepú, quilineja — which give unique and unrepeatable colors.
craft.p3: Every piece is finished with hand embroidery: flowers, birds, and Chilote motifs that make each garment a work of art.

newsletter.title: Ayenantú Updates
newsletter.sub: New pieces, workshop stories and launches.

product.careText: Hand wash in warm water · Neutral soap · Do not spin · Natural drying
product.materialText: 100% virgin sheep wool · Natural herbal dyes

success.title: Thank you for your purchase!
success.sub: You will receive a confirmation email shortly.

about.title: Our Story
about.p1: Ayenantú — 'smiling sun' in Mapudungun — was born in Quellón, at the southern tip of Chiloé, from the hands of María Ruth.
about.p2: It all started with a wooden loom inherited from her grandmother and a desire to keep alive the textile tradition of the archipelago. Over the years, María Ruth perfected the art of loom weaving, hand embroidery and artisan felting.
about.p3: Today, every Ayenantú garment is made entirely by hand: spun, woven, dyed with native island plants and embroidered with Chilote motifs. No two pieces are the same.
about.p4: When you buy Ayenantú, you support a local artisan and carry with you an authentic piece of Chiloé culture.

care.title: Wool Care Guide
care.intro: Virgin wool deserves special care to preserve its natural properties.

steps.title: Hand wash
steps.desc: Use warm water (max 30°C). Gently submerge without rubbing.

steps.title: Neutral soap
steps.desc: Use neutral soap or mild shampoo. Avoid enzyme detergents.

steps.title: No spin
steps.desc: Do not machine wash or spin dry. Gently press to remove water.

steps.title: Natural drying
steps.desc: Dry flat on a towel, away from direct sun and heat.

steps.title: Low iron
steps.desc: Iron on low temperature with a damp cloth between iron and garment.

steps.title: Storage
steps.desc: Store folded in a dry place. Avoid hangers for heavy wool garments.
